title: Testing Genuine Multipartite Nonlocality via an Inflated Network with Multicopy Entangled States
summary: Understanding the nonlocality of multipartite quantum systems provides valuable insights into their behaviors and potential applications. In this Letter, assuming a quantum network inflated with multiple copies of genuine multipartite entangled states, we propose a novel noise-robust approach to tes…

authors: N. J. Ayres et al. (nEDM Collaboration at PSI)
title: Ultracold Neutron Energy Spectrum and Storage Properties from Magnetically Induced Spin Depolarization
summary: We present a novel method for extracting the energy spectrum of ultracold neutrons from magnetically induced spin depolarization measurements using the n2EDM apparatus. This method is also used to exploit sensitivity to the storage properties of the materials used to trap ultracold neutrons, specifi…

title: Programmable Assembly of Ground State Fermionic Tweezer Arrays
summary: We demonstrate deterministic preparation of arbitrary two-component product states of fermionic $^{6}\mathrm{Li}$ atoms in an $8×8$ optical tweezer array, achieving motional ground-state fidelities above 98.5%. Leveraging the large differential magnetic moments for spin-resolution, with parallelized…

title: Acoustic Analogy of Quantum Baldin Sum Rule for Optimal Causal Scattering
summary: Sound wave scattering can be increased in one frequency range only by reducing scattering in another range, according to experiments—a discovery relevant for acoustic engineering.

title: Persistence of the Berezinskii-Kosterlitz-Thouless Transition with Long-Range Couplings
summary: The Berezinskii-Kosterlitz-Thouless (BKT) transition is an archetypal example of a topological phase transition, which is driven by the proliferation of vortices. In this Letter, we analyze the persistence of the BKT transition in the XY model under the influence of long-range algebraically decaying…

title: Realizing Unitary $k$-Designs with a Single Quench
summary: We present a single-quench protocol that generates unitary $k$-designs with minimal control. A system first evolves under a random Hamiltonian ${H}_{1}$; at a switch time ${t}_{s}≥{t}_{\mathrm{Th}}$ (the Thouless time), it is quenched to an independently drawn ${H}_{2}$ from the same ensemble and th…

title: Breakdown of Disorder-Suppressed Floquet Heating under Two-Frequency Driving
summary: Periodic (Floquet) driving enables Hamiltonian engineering and nonequilibrium phases, but interacting systems eventually heat by absorbing energy from the drive. Disorder can greatly delay this process, yielding long-lived prethermal plateaus. Here, we show that this protection can fail when pulse-t…

title: Crossed Surface Flat Bands in Three-Dimensional Superconducting Altermagnets
summary: Higher dimensional topological phases in three-dimensional superconducting altermagnets characterized by zero-energy surface crossed flat bands indicate the interplay between altermagnetic and superconducting symmetries.

title: Nonlinear Magnetoelectric Edelstein Effect
summary: The generation of spin magnetization by linear and nonlinear Edelstein effects has so far relied solely on electric fields. Here, we propose a distinct mechanism, the nonlinear magnetoelectric Edelstein effect (NMEE), in which electric and magnetic fields act cooperatively to produce spin magnetizat…

title: Observation of Acoustic Magnetochiral Anisotropy in $α$ Quartz
summary: Acoustic magnetochiral anisotropy is observed in a diamagnetic crystal of quartz.

title: Acoustic Bound Pair States in the Continuum Induced by Off-Site Two-Body Interactions
summary: A novel many-body bound state in the continuum induced entirely by uniform off-site 2-body interactions is observed by mapping a quantum 1D Bose-Hubbard model onto a 2D macroscopic phononic crystal.

title: Beating Hermitian Speed Limits for Entanglement Generation via Exceptional Points in a Trapped-Ion System
summary: Entanglement generation is a cornerstone of quantum information science, yet its speed in Hermitian systems is fundamentally constrained by the coupling strength, a restriction known as the quantum speed limit. Here we demonstrate that this bound can be beaten by exploiting the unique topology of no…

title: Quantum Transition Rates in Arbitrary Physical Processes
summary: A theory of quantum transition rates refines the concept of quantum speed limits.

title: Quantum Mpemba Effect Induced by Non-Markovian Exceptional Points
summary: Quantum Mpemba effect describes an anomalous phenomenon of accelerated relaxation, which is of fundamental interest in the field of nonequilibrium thermodynamics. Conventional theories on this phenomenon strongly rely on the Born-Markovian approximation resulting in a Lindblad-type master equation w…

title: Adaptively Secure Unitary Designs with Constant Non-Clifford Cost
summary: Randomness is a fundamental resource in quantum information, with crucial applications in cryptography, algorithms, and error correction. A central challenge is to construct unitary $k$ designs that closely approximate Haar-random unitaries while minimizing the costly use of non-Clifford operations.…

title: Physical Mechanism behind the Early Onset of the Ultimate State in Supergravitational Centrifugal Thermal Convection
summary: We present a combined experimental and numerical investigation of the transition from the classical to the ultimate regime of thermal turbulence in a supergravitational centrifugal convection system. The transition is found to be robust, with the critical Rayleigh number decreasing systematically as…

title: Generalized Scaling of Focal Temperature in Converging Shock Waves
summary: A scaling framework unifying the markedly different and independently studied cylindrical and spherical shock convergence is presented. For ionizing argon, we show that the focal temperature becomes invariant to shock symmetry and initial shock conditions when scaled by the prefocus shock Mach numbe…

title: Are Neural Networks Collision Resistant?
summary: A study of the computational complexity of finding collisions in a simple one-layer neural network shows that efficient algorithms fail to find collisions, a result of direct relevance for the definition of new cryptographic protocols based on machine-learning models.

title: Reassessing the Boundary between Classical and Nonclassical for Individual Quantum Processes
summary: Researchers have proposed a unified rule to distinguish quantum from classical processes, recovering many standard signatures of quantumness while revealing that many phenomena long thought to be classical actually possess hidden, intrinsically quantum properties that could power future technologies.

title: Super-resonance: Breaking the Bandwidth Limit of Resonant Modes and Its Application to Flow Control
summary: Super-resonance maintains a broadband out-of-phase response well beyond the characteristic bandwidth of conventional resonance, enabling broadband flow stabilization among other applications.

title: Anyon Superfluidity of Excitons in Quantum Hall Bilayers
summary: Theoretical analysis of quantum Hall bilayers reveals that topological criticality facilitates the emergence of anyonic exciton superfluids, offering a robust mechanism for engineering exotic collective quantum states.

title: High-Rate Discrete-Modulated Continuous-Variable Quantum Key Distribution with Composable Security
summary: Researchers achieve a high secret key rate for quantum communication over fiber optics. By combining advanced signal modulation with new security analysis tools, they have made highly secure, high-speed quantum networks closer to practical implementation.

title: Nondestructive Optical Readout and Manipulation of Circular Rydberg Atoms
summary: Local quantum nondemolition measurements and optical manipulation of long-lived circular Rydberg atoms are demonstrated by coupling them to an auxiliary array of low-angular-momentum Rydberg atoms.

title: Erratum: Opposite Effects of the Rotational and Translational Energy on the Rates of Ion-Molecule Reactions near 0 K: The ${\mathrm{D}}_{2}^{+}+{\mathrm{NH}}_{3}$ and ${\mathrm{D}}_{2}^{+}+{\mathrm{ND}}_{3}$ Reactions [Phys. Rev. X 14, 011034 (2024)]
summary: [Phys. Rev. X 16, 029901] Published Wed May 20, 2026

title: Stochastic Calculus for Pathwise Observables of Markov-Jump Processes: Unification of Diffusion and Jump Dynamics
summary: A complete stochastic calculus for pathwise observables of Markov-jump processes is developed, unifying, in the continuum limit, the previously disjoint theories of diffusion and jump processes.

title: Correlated Phase Error Bursts in a Gap-Engineered Superconducting Qubit Array
summary: Ionizing radiation is shown to induce a new type of correlated error in a superconducting quantum processor—phase error bursts—that undermines quantum error correction even with state-of-the-art radiation protection.

title: Entropy of Strongly Correlated Electrons in a Partially Filled Landau Level
summary: Entropy measurements in partially filled Landau levels reveal the successive freezing of spin and motional degrees of freedom, reaching sensitivities that constrain the isolation of topological entropy in non-Abelian quantum phases.

title: Polar and quadratic magneto-optical Kerr effects in nonmagnetic/ferromagnet bilayers for spin-orbit torque measurements
summary: Spin orbit torque is one of the key technologies in developing next-generation spintronic devices. Various approaches have been developed to characterize the efficiency of spin orbit torque. Recent studies suggest that the optical approach provides a better means to study the efficiency compared to the conventional electrical approach. Here, the authors clarify the reason behind this. It is the small quadratic component of the magneto-optical Kerr effect compared to its polar counterpart that defines the high accuracy.

title: Controlling resonant spin photocurrent using magnetic field: Application to the magnetoelectric oxide ${\mathrm{Cr}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{3}$
summary: Controlling magnons in magnetic insulators is key to their application in spintronics. In this work, we study the magnon spin photocurrent in effective spin models for ${\mathrm{Cr}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{3}$, a material known for its magnetoelectric (ME) effect. Using the nonlinear response theory, we s…

title: Magnetic field induced modification of a first-order ferromagnetic transition in ${\mathrm{Eu}}_{2}\mathrm{In}$
summary: We present a comprehensive study of the temperature- and magnetic-field-dependent magnetization, specific heat, and local crystal structure across the first-order ferromagnetic–paramagnetic transition in ${\mathrm{Eu}}_{2}\mathrm{In}$. Anomalies in the magnetocaloric response are observed near $H≈25…

title: Fragile long-range antiferromagnetic order in the triangular-lattice quantum magnet ${\mathrm{ErZn}}_{3}{\mathrm{P}}_{3}$
summary: Rare-earth triangular lattices provide an ideal platform for exploring novel quantum magnetic states. Here, we report the crystal growth and magnetic properties of a rare-earth triangular-lattice compound, ${\mathrm{ErZn}}_{3}{\mathrm{P}}_{3}$. Alternating-current magnetic susceptibility and specifi…

title: Ramp Josephson junctions of Al/Ti/$\mathrm{S}{\mathrm{r}}_{2}\mathrm{Ru}{\mathrm{O}}_{4}$: Observation of single-domain quantum oscillations and the detection of chiral surface current
summary: The determination of how the phase of the superconducting order parameter in a superconductor varies with the spatial direction, which can be done only through Josephson-effect-based phase-sensitive measurements, is crucial for the establishment of the precise pairing symmetry of the superconductor.…

title: Spin-triplet pairing and spin supercurrent in $p$-wave magnetic superconductors
summary: Spin-triplet superconductors are promising platforms for dissipationless spin transport, which typically originates from the interplay of ferromagnetism and superconductivity. However, ferromagnetism, in turn, suppresses superconductivity. As a class of unconventional magnetic materials, $p$-wave ma…
